title: ipv6_addr.c: ULA prefix check is stricter than RFC 4193 (fd00::/8 vs fc00::/7)
description:
## Location
firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/ipv6_addr.c:145

## Issue
lichen_make_ula() rejects prefixes that are not in fd00::/8:

```c
/* Check prefix is in fd00::/8 */
if (prefix[0] != 0xfd) {
    LOG_ERR("ULA prefix must be in fd00::/8, got %02x", prefix[0]);
    return -EINVAL;
}
```

RFC 4193 defines Unique Local Addresses as fc00::/7, which includes both fc00::/8 and fd00::/8. The fc00::/8 range is reserved for future use but is technically part of the ULA space. Some implementations may use fc00::/8 prefixes.

## What goes wrong
- Prefixes in fc00::/8 are rejected even though they are valid ULAs per RFC 4193
- A future RFC may assign meaning to fc00::/8, breaking interop with nodes using those prefixes
- Error message says fd00::/8 which is technically correct for current IANA guidance but inconsistent with RFC language

## Fix
Either:
1. Widen the check to accept fc00::/7: if ((prefix[0] & 0xFE) != 0xFC)
2. Add a comment explaining LICHEN intentionally restricts to fd00::/8 per current IANA guidance and the python implementation behavior
